title: Verilation fails with --hierarchical option when sources provided via -f option
body:
Attached is a simple example: top module contains instantiation of another module. The inner module marked as hierarchical.
When trying to build the example with --hierarchical option and passing the sources via -f option then verilation fails:
`$ verilator --hierarchical -f source.f -cc --top-module add_4 `
The following error message is produced.

```
make: Entering directory '/home/garik/microchip/verilator/adder_4/hierarchical/obj_dir'
make[1]: Entering directory '/home/garik/microchip/verilator/adder_4/hierarchical'
/opt/verilator/bin/verilator -f obj_dir/Vadd_2_8__hierMkArgs.f
%Error: verilator: No Input Verilog file specified on command line, see verilator --help for more information
make[1]: *** [obj_dir/Vadd_4_hier.mk:30: hier_launch_verilator] Error 1
make[1]: Leaving directory '/home/garik/microchip/verilator/adder_4/hierarchical'
make: *** [Vadd_4_hier.mk:39: Vadd_2_8/add_2_8.sv] Error 2
make: Leaving directory '/home/garik/microchip/verilator/adder_4/hierarchical/obj_dir'
%Error: make -C obj_dir -f Vadd_4_hier.mk  -j 1  hier_verilation exited with 2
%Error: Command Failed ulimit -s unlimited 2>/dev/null; exec /opt/verilator/bin/verilator_bin --hierarchical -f source.f -cc --top-module add_4
```

Note, when I modify the generated obj_dir/Vadd_4_hier.mk file and edit the hier_launch_verilator target by adding "-f sources.f" at the end of the recipe line, and execute make like this:
`$ make -C obj_dir -f Vadd_4_hier.mk hier_verilation`
then the verilation is successful.

The Verilator version is  5.031, master branch (6e204ed0dda3f51ccd185ff144a6d7ef5b9f1239).
OS is Ubuntu 22.04.


[verilator_mk_issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/18479089/verilator_mk_issue.zip)
